growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Karita et al. Scale-dependent tipping points of bacterial colonization resistance. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2115496119. [PMC8851462]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum growth rate was calculated by fitting an exponential curve to the initial 2-h growth. The growth rate of A. indonesiensis was measured as 0.325 ± .003 h-1 (SI Appendix, Fig. S4A).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate directly reported from plate-reader OD600 exponential-fit assay (30°C, MRS, batch 96-well). doubling_time_h calculated as ln2/0.325=2.13 h (not stated verbatim but simple derived conversion of the reported rate).

growth_records.cite: Karita et al. Scale-dependent tipping points of bacterial colonization resistance. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2115496119. [PMC8851462]
growth_records.snippet: We first cultured cells at 22 °C, where the growth rate is 0.28 h-1, until a steady state and ramped up the temperature to 30 °C, where the growth rate is 0.33 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate directly reported (measured via particle image velocimetry of jammed population in microfluidic chamber, continuous fresh-medium supply, not a defined chemostat dilution rate). doubling_time_h=ln2/0.28=2.48 h derived. Organism identity (A. indonesiensis SB003) inferred from Methods section describing this as the strain used throughout the jamming/microfluidic experiments; not explicitly re-stated in this exact sentence.

growth_records.cite: Karita et al. Scale-dependent tipping points of bacterial colonization resistance. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2115496119. [PMC8851462]
growth_records.snippet: ramped up the temperature to 30 °C, where the growth rate is 0.33 h-1 (the growth rate measurement is shown in SI Appendix, Fig. S4D). ... the doubling time of the cell (2.1 h)
growth_records.notes: Growth rate directly reported (PIV method, microfluidic chamber, 30°C). doubling_time_h=2.1 h matches paper's own separately stated value (ln2/0.33=2.10), confirming internal consistency; growth_method set to reported since both mu and Td are explicit in text.
